name: Giblet Gravy with Wine
recipeIngredient: 1 small Celery stalk (with leaves), coarsely chopped
Giblets and neck from turkey, coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the first seven ingredients with salt and pepper to taste in a medium saucepan. Partially cover and bring to a simmer. The stock should reduce to about 1 1/2 cups and have an intense flavor.
2. When the roasted turkey has been transferred to a platter, degrease the juices in the roasting pan. Place the pan on high heat. Pour the reduced stock through a strainer into the pan juices. Bring to a boil and cook for about 2 minutes, stirring and scraping up any browned bits that cling to the pan.
3. Beat together the remaining water, Madeira, and flour until smooth. Stir into the boiling pan juices and continue cooking until the gravy is thickened, about 5 minutes. Taste; if the sauce seems weak, continue reduction. Add seasoning as needed.
4. To serve, pour the gravy into a heated serving bowl. The gravy can be prepared up to 1 day in advance through step one. Any leftover gravy can be refrigerated for up to 5 days or frozen for up to 3 months in an airtight container.
